data.title: The core principles of cryptocurrency value: A review of leading players in major profit-generating sectors
data.description:
Author: Stacy Muur Translator: Shan Ouba, Jinse Finance

The initial "fat protocol" theory held that value in the cryptocurrency space would tilt towards the underlying public chains, rather than various applications. However, this framework no longer holds true.

In 2025, I observed that value was flowing towards various "value control points": user interfaces that control user needs, trading platforms that aggregate liquidity, issuers that hold balance sheets, and network layers that profit from market inefficiencies. Regardless of which public chain ultimately prevails, which application becomes popular, or which narrative dominates, these entities can continuously earn transaction fee revenue.

This ranking combines multiple indicators such as revenue, user scale, average revenue per user (ARPU), market share, and capital efficiency to analyze the actual value flow in the current market, clarifying which ecosystem layers possess "value depth," the underlying reasons, and the next direction of marginal value flow.

I. Leading Wallets Leader: Phantom Annualized Revenue: Approximately $105 million (Q3 2025: Quarterly revenue approximately $35 million, annualized) Monthly Active Users: Approximately 15 million Average Annual Revenue Per User: Approximately $7.00 Market Position: Holds approximately 39% of the Solana wallet market share Performance and Theoretical Alignment: Phantom, by controlling the entry point of user needs, has become... The leading user-facing wallet in the Solana ecosystem. This wallet covers diverse scenarios including token exchange, NFT trading, perpetual contracts, and payments, enabling the monetization of user behavior before its value is transferred to decentralized exchanges or other protocols. Within weeks of its launch, Phantom's perpetual contracts achieved a trading volume exceeding $1 billion, demonstrating the wallet's transformation from a passive interactive interface to a proactive financial services platform. In January 2025, Phantom completed a $150 million Series C funding round, reaching a valuation of $3 billion, reflecting market recognition of its transformation strategy. Following closely behind are the following competitors: MetaMask: Expanding monetization channels by integrating perpetual contracts and token exchange functionality, and launching a $30 million LINEA ecosystem incentive program to further deepen user ecosystem stickiness. Trust Wallet: With over 200 million total downloads and having blocked $162 million in fraud attacks, demonstrating strong user reach, but its average revenue per user is relatively weak.

II. Leading Public Chains

Leader: Ethereum

  • Protocol Annualized Revenue: Approximately $300 million

  • Monthly Active Users: Approximately 8.6 million

  • Average Annual Revenue per User: Approximately $30-35

Performance and Theoretical Alignment:
Ethereum remains the core settlement layer in the cryptocurrency field. Its value does not stem from its high-throughput client-side transaction processing capabilities, but rather from its role as the final arbiter for high-value transactions, MEV withdrawals, stablecoin circulation, and various rolling network and institutional financial settlements. Ethereum's fee revenue comes from MEV (Mean Exchange Value), block space fees, and settlement requirements, rather than simply the number of transactions. This characteristic structurally slows its growth compared to public chains focused on transaction execution, but with continued capital inflows, its competitive advantage is becoming increasingly robust. Following closely behind are: Solana: A leading transaction execution public chain, with peak monthly revenue reaching $240 million. Its growth is driven by meme trading, perpetual contracts, and various user-facing applications. The implementation of performance upgrades such as Firedancer and Alpenglow further solidifies its momentum. Base: The fastest-growing Ethereum Layer 2 network by activity level, with transaction volume achieving triple-digit growth month-over-month. Uniswap's cumulative transaction volume has surpassed $200 billion, making it the core transaction execution platform for Ethereum users.

III. Leading Decentralized Exchanges for Perpetual Contracts

Leader: Hyperliquid

  • Annualized Revenue: Approximately $950-1 billion

  • Open Interest: Approximately $6.5 billion

  • 30-Day Perpetual Contract Trading Volume: Approximately $225 Billion

Performance and Theoretical Fit: Perpetual contracts are the most profitable basic product in the cryptocurrency field, and Hyperliquid firmly occupies a dominant position in this sector.

Performance and Theoretical Fit: Perpetual contracts are the most profitable basic product in the cryptocurrency field, and Hyperliquid firmly occupies a dominant position in this field ... This platform utilizes a dedicated public blockchain to achieve integrated aggregation of liquidity, transaction execution, and order flow. It eliminates MEV loss and order routing fragmentation issues during transactions, thus achieving efficient fee capture. In July 2025 alone, Hyperliquid's fee revenue accounted for approximately 35% of the total revenue of all blockchain protocols, and its token buyback scale ranked first among all crypto projects, making it a best practice example of the "thick trading platform" theory. Challenger: Lighter: Rapidly accumulated users from its initial launch, with cumulative trading volume exceeding $1 trillion and monthly trading volume reaching $300 billion, but its profit margin is relatively low. Drift: Cumulative trading volume has exceeded $2 trillion, total value locked (TVL) is approximately $3.2 billion, and revenue is approximately $49 million. It has strong growth momentum, but its market dominance is slightly weaker.

IV. Leading Lending Protocols

Leader: Aave

  • Annualized Revenue: Approximately $115 million

  • Monthly Active Users: Approximately 120,000

  • Total Value Locked: Approximately $32-35 billion

  • Capital Utilization: Approximately 40%

Performance and Theoretical Alignment:

Aave is a leading institutional-grade balance sheet lending platform in DeFi. ... Although the profit margin of lending is structurally lower than that of trading platforms, Aave has formed a unique competitive advantage due to its scale, risk resistance, and high stickiness of institutional funds. In 2025, the protocol's cumulative deposits exceeded $3 trillion, and active lending reached $29 billion. While the accumulation of "value depth" in the lending sector is slow, it possesses extremely strong defensive capabilities. Challenger: Fluid: A leading liquidity protocol with a total cross-chain locked value of $5-6 billion, ranking third in the industry in lending and second in monthly active users. It also provides efficient trading support for decentralized exchanges, with related trading volume reaching $150 billion and generating over $23 million in fees. Morpho: With deposits exceeding $10 billion, it is the largest deposit protocol in the Base ecosystem, indicating that the lending business is transforming towards modularization and marketization. V. Leading Real-World Asset Tokenization Protocols Leader: BlackRock BUIDL Assets Under Management: Approximately $2.3 billion Yield: Approximately 4% (Tokenized US Treasury Products) Number of Holders: Less than 100 (All Institutional Investors) Performance and Theoretical Alignment: The "value depth" of the RWA tokenization track stems from scale and trust, not the number of users. BUIDL has completed deployments across seven public chains, and its related products have been included in the collateral scope of centralized platforms, becoming a structural bridge connecting traditional finance and on-chain finance. This refers to the "value depth" of the balance sheet, not the value accumulation for the user end. Following closely behind are: Ondo Finance: Total value locked has exceeded $1 billion, and it has received approval from the EU's Crypto Asset Market Regulation (MiCA), becoming a leading crypto-native real-world asset tokenization platform.

VI. Leading Liquidity Staking Derivatives / Restaking Protocol Layers

Leader: EigenLayer

  • Restaking Asset Size: Approximately $12.4 billion

  • Annualized Fee Revenue: Approximately $70 million

  • User Base: Approximately 300,000-400,000

Performance and Theoretical Alignment:
EigenLayer is an infrastructure-level protocol in the restaking field, commercializing it by renting out Ethereum's security mechanisms to various Active Validation Services (AVS). The launch of EigenCloud (including EigenAI and EigenCompute) expands its business scope into the field of verifiable off-chain computing. The "value depth" of EigenLayer stems from its strong ecosystem appeal as a protocol. Challenger: Ether.fi: Annualized revenue of approximately $100 million, actively conducting ETHFI token buybacks, and achieving efficient commercial monetization for users through its Cash product.

VII. Leading Aggregators / Routing Protocol Layer

Leader: Jupiter

  • Protocol Annualized Revenue: Approximately $12 million

  • Decentralized Exchange Aggregator 30-Day Trading Volume: Approximately $46 billion

  • Market Share: Accounts for approximately 90% of the traffic of aggregators in the Solana ecosystem

Performance and Theoretical Alignment:
The core of aggregator profitability lies in the trading decision-making process. Jupiter captures value by controlling trading routing, pricing, and execution quality, intercepting profits before liquidity providers obtain the spread. Challenger: COWSwap: With a cumulative trading volume exceeding $1.1 trillion, it possesses strong MEV protection capabilities and is particularly favored by institutional traders.

VIII. Leading Stablecoin Issuers

Leader: Tether (USDT)

  • Circulating Supply: Approximately $185 billion

  • Annualized Revenue: Over $10 billion

  • Treasury Holdings: Approximately $135 billion

Performance and Theoretical Alignment:
Tether is the most profitable entity in the cryptocurrency field. Stablecoin issuers earn interest income by holding US Treasury bonds, realizing the commercialization of floating funds. Their business model is structurally superior to most crypto protocols. This is the purest practical example of the "thick balance sheet" theory. Challengers: USDC (issued by Circle): Circulating supply of approximately $78 billion, rapid growth, but relatively low profit margins. Ethena USDe: Circulating supply of approximately $12 billion, representing a new competitive model for stablecoins based on synthetic yields.

IX. Leading Prediction Markets

Leader: Polymarket

  • Annualized Revenue: Not Disclosed

  • Monthly Trading Volume: Approximately $1.5-2 billion (peaks during major events)

  • Monthly Active Traders: Approximately 200,000-300,000

Performance and Theoretical Alignment:
The core profitability of prediction markets lies in user attention and market uncertainty. Their core structural advantage lies in information attraction—liquidity converges on platforms perceived as having the most accurate pricing. Once a positive cycle of "accurate pricing - attracting liquidity" is formed, competitors will find it difficult to accumulate sufficient market depth. Polymarket's "value depth" doesn't stem from continued user engagement, but rather from its status as a globally influential "consensus bellwether," a level of attention with immense commercial value. Prediction markets represent a new "value depth" in the crypto space, characterized by: not relying on total value locked; not bearing directional market risk; high fee elasticity during major events; and strong narrative dissemination capabilities (market probability data itself can become news hotspots). This characteristic makes it one of the few crypto applications that can benefit from macroeconomic and political fluctuations.

Strong Challenger:

  • Kalshi: Regulated by the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), it supports dollar-denominated event trading (such as sporting events and political elections), and its trading volume exceeds that of Polymarket at certain times, attracting the attention of traditional financial institutions. However, it is currently at a disadvantage in terms of crypto-native liquidity.

    Strong Challenger:

    • Kalshi: Regulated by the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), it supports dollar-denominated event trading (such as sporting events and political elections). Its ...

    10. Leading MEV Protocol Layers

    Leader: Flashbots

    • Annualized MEV Extraction: Approximately $230 million

    • Cumulative MEV Managed: Over $1.5 billion

    Performance and Theoretical Alignment MEV is the implicit cost of blockchain space. Flashbots institutionalizes the MEV extraction and allocation process, becoming an indispensable infrastructure for Ethereum and various rolling networks.

Performance and Theoretical Alignment MEV is the implicit cost of blockchain space. Flashbots institutionalizes the MEV extraction and allocation process, becoming an indispensable infrastructure for Ethereum and various rolling networks.

Emerging Competitors: Jito: In Q1 2025, it captured approximately 66% of the Solana ecosystem's transaction fee revenue through MEV tips and the Block Space Auction (BAM) mechanism. Arbitrum: Since its launch, it has generated approximately $10 million in transaction fees, marking the expansion of MEV commercialization into higher layers of the ecosystem.

data.title: Polygon Shifts from Layer-2 to Regulated Payment Platform In The United States After Acquiring Coinme And Sequence
data.description:
Polygon Jumps From Layer-2 To Regulated Payments

Polygon, long known for powering Ethereum’s scalability and supporting DeFi and NFT ecosystems, is pivoting from being purely a blockchain infrastructure provider to a fully regulated crypto payments platform in the United States. The platform now aims to make everyday crypto payments and stablecoin transfers fully compliant with U.S. financial rules, signalling a major step toward mainstream adoption.

BREAKING: Polygon to become U.S. regulated payments platform

We’re acquiring Coinme and Sequence to move all money onchain.
→ Regulated money movement in 48 states
→ Fiat on/off ramps
→ 50,000 fiat-to-crypto locations in the U.S.
→ Easy onboarding with wallet infra
→… pic.twitter.com/lwvLheEc3P

— Polygon | POL (@0xPolygon) January 13, 2026

Polygon’s pivot relies on two strategic acquisitions. The purchase of Coinme, a U.S.-based regulated crypto payments firm which gives Polygon access to existing money transmission licenses and the acquisition of Sequence, a blockchain payment infrastructure specialist, strengthens its technical stack for operating regulated payment flows.

Bread talks about Polygon acquiring Coinme and Sequence

"It is the first time a chain co. has bought a regulated entity to his knowledge"

"It was actually kind of groundbreaking" pic.twitter.com/aMXBUFsqim

— Arjun (@clipsofcrypto) January 14, 2026

According to Polygon Labs’ CEO, the acquisition of these two companies provide the “missing pieces” necessary for it to deliver complete, end-to-end regulated crypto payments.

Through these moves, Polygon now holds money transmitter licenses in 48 U.S. states, enabling it to offer large-scale fiat-to-crypto conversion and services compliant with KYC and AML standards. These licenses are crucial because they legally allow Polygon to operate as a payments platform, ensuring customer verification and anti-fraud safeguards are enforced across its transactions.

Stablecoins at the heart of the strategy

At the core of Polygon’s payments push are stablecoins, which provide a predictable and low-volatility medium of exchange for both consumer and business transactions. The platform aims to harness the rapid growth of stablecoin adoption, especially for cross-border payments and B2B settlements, where volatility from assets like Bitcoin or Ether would be a barrier.

🔥 HUGE: Stablecoin market cap hits a new record high of $310 billion, marking a 70% surge in just one year, signaling a fundamental shift in crypto adoption.

What's driving this explosive growth? pic.twitter.com/0qHAaVzIBi

— Cointelegraph (@Cointelegraph) December 26, 2025

By embedding stablecoin rails within a regulated framework, Polygon positions itself to serve not only crypto-native users but also traditional financial institutions and fintech companies seeking compliant on-chain infrastructure. This approach aligns with Polygon’s long-term vision of an “Open Money Stack”, enabling value to flow as seamlessly as data across applications and networks.

Polygon’s U.S. focus is also strategic. As the world’s largest financial market with stringent regulatory standards, establishing a compliant footprint in the U.S. provides credibility and opens access to a broad market of institutional and retail participants.

stablecoins now account for 1.35% of the total U.S. dollar supply pic.twitter.com/PVSiMvvdCC

— haonan (@haonan) December 30, 2025

Furthermore, recent regulatory clarity for stablecoins under the GENIUS Act creates a framework for “payment stablecoins,” specifying authorized issuers and operational safeguards. By building in the U.S., Polygon positions itself as a front-runner in regulated crypto payments, ready to capitalize on a booming market at the intersection of blockchain and traditional finance.

data.title: Banks Is Taking Over "Crypto Exchanges" In Europe. How Does This Work?
data.description:
Banks, not exchanges, are rolling out crypto in Europe

DZ Bank, Germany’s second-largest banking group by assets, has secured regulatory approval under the European Union’s Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — a move that underscores a broader shift underway in Europe’s crypto market: traditional banks are emerging as the main architects of regulated crypto access, rather than crypto-native exchanges.

JUST IN

🇩🇪 Germany’s second largest bank, DZ Bank, gets approval to offer Bitcoin and crypto trading.

TradFi keeps stepping in 👀 pic.twitter.com/edUg5Yp8vl

— Crypto Verse (@CryptoVerse_Co) January 14, 2026

The license, granted by Germany’s financial regulator BaFin, authorizes DZ Bank to operate its “meinKrypto” platform — not as a consumer-facing exchange, but as centralized crypto infrastructure for Germany’s nationwide cooperative banking network.

Rather than competing directly with crypto exchanges, DZ Bank is rolling out crypto services to hundreds of local banks, so they too can provide it to their customers within a fully regulated banking environment. The approach reflects how MiCA is reshaping crypto adoption across Europe: crypto is being absorbed into existing banking rails, not built outside of them.

A hub-and-spoke model for bank-led crypto access

Under the operating structure, DZ Bank acts as the central platform operator, providing the trading, custody, and compliance infrastructure, while individual cooperative banks decide independently whether to offer crypto services to their retail clients.

Each participating bank must still submit a separate MiCA notification to BaFin before activating crypto trading — a safeguard that preserves local control while maintaining centralized regulatory standards. This hub-and-spoke model allows crypto to scale through the banking system without forcing uniform adoption across all institutions.

At launch, meinKrypto will support Bitcoin, Ether, Litecoin, and Cardano, accessible directly through the VR Banking App as a self-directed investment option.

Germany’s 2nd-largest bank just went full crypto. 🇩🇪 DZ Bank launches meinKrypto under MiCAR approval — offering BTC, ETH, ADA & LTC directly to retail customers.#Germany#Bitcoin#Ethereum#Cardano#Litecoinhttps://t.co/uhqX9c6YBz

— TheUnhashed (@theunhashed) January 15, 2026

The platform was a project jointly developed with Atruvia, the IT provider for Germany’s cooperative banking sector, ensuring scalability and regulatory alignment across hundreds of institutions.

Why this matters for Europe’s crypto landscape

DZ Bank’s approval highlights a defining feature of Europe’s crypto trajectory under MiCA: regulated banks — not exchanges — are becoming the primary distribution channel for digital assets.

Unlike crypto-native platforms, banks already possess customer relationships, compliance infrastructure, and balance-sheet capacity. MiCA gives them the legal certainty to integrate crypto gradually, starting with spot trading and custody before expanding into more complex use cases.

While meinKrypto does not currently offer lending, industry observers note that regulated trading and custody are prerequisites for bank-led crypto lending, particularly where assets may later be used as collateral or held on balance sheets. MiCA establishes the guardrails needed for such products to emerge within traditional finance.

From pilots to execution under MiCA

The license builds on DZ Bank’s September 2024 partnership with Boerse Stuttgart Digital, which aimed to roll out crypto trading and custody services across roughly 700 cooperative banks, pending regulatory clearance.

JUST IN: 🇩🇪 Germany’s €370 BILLION DekaBank is launching Bitcoin and crypto trading + custody for institutions.

Their government sells BTC. Their citizens stack it. pic.twitter.com/ogRM96nH2Y

— Dear Bitcoiner ⚡️ (@DearBitcoiner) February 25, 2025

With MiCA authorization now in place, those plans can move from experimentation to execution — positioning DZ Bank among the first major European institutions to operationalize the regulation at scale.

As more EU banks follow suit, MiCA is steadily transforming crypto from a parallel financial system into an embedded feature of Europe’s regulated banking ecosystem, with banks — not exchanges — increasingly setting the terms of adoption.

data.title: Bitpanda Drops London Over Low Liquidity and Opts for Frankfurt IPO in 2026
data.description:
Bitpanda Targets Frankfurt For A High Stakes Public Listing

Bitpanda started in a Vienna office in 2014 and is now ready to face the intense scrutiny of the Frankfurt Stock Exchange.

The company is finalising plans to go public in the first half of 2026, seeking a valuation between 4 billion euros and 5 billion euros.

📣 NEW: Peter Thiel-backed #Bitpanda GmbH is preparing for an IPO on the Frankfurt stock exchange, potentially as early as the first half of 2026. pic.twitter.com/3ufCGUWuvX

— Coinpaper (@coinpapercom) January 14, 2026

This transition from a private fintech unicorn to a listed entity marks a new era for the European crypto market.

While the listing could happen as early as the first quarter, the company is currently working with major banks including Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, and Deutsche Bank AG to manage the process.

Although final decisions on the exact timing are still being discussed, the internal push for a listing is moving quickly as the platform looks to solidify its status as a top player in the region.

Why Bitpanda Chose Frankfurt Over London And New York

Choosing the right venue for an IPO is a strategic gamble, and for Bitpanda, London was simply not an attractive enough option.

Co-founder Eric Demuth had previously ruled out the London Stock Exchange because of concerns regarding market depth.

The former Bitpanda chief during an interview explained,

“Everyone is moving away from the LSE right now.”

He pointed to other fintech firms like Wise, which recently moved its primary listing from London to New York, as a sign of the current trend.

📉 Bitpanda has abandoned its IPO in London

The cryptocurrency exchange Bitpanda will not go public in London, citing the low liquidity of the London Stock Exchange.

According to the company's co-founder Eric Demuth, the listing may take place in Frankfurt or New York, but the… pic.twitter.com/KFtrkC1PEd

— Crypto Research Network (@CrResearchNet) August 26, 2025

While New York was also considered, Bitpanda eventually settled on Frankfurt to lean into its identity as a dominant European firm.

With a 60 percent market share in Austria, the company is positioning itself as a local champion that understands the regulatory landscape of the European Union better than its international rivals.

Will Recent Profitability Support A Five Billion Euro Valuation

Investors will be looking closely at Bitpanda’s books as it prepares for the 2026 debut.

The company reported a significant jump in revenue for 2024, reaching 393 million euros compared to 147.6 million euros the year before.

🚨 @Bitpanda IS GOING HUGE 🚨

✅Bitpanda hits €393 million in 2024 revenue.
✅Plans UK and European expansion.
✅Regulatory approvals strengthen regional presence.

Are you guys familiar with this exchange yet? 👀 pic.twitter.com/ekc1B9dIxG

— Crypto Virtuos (@CryptoVirtuos) August 5, 2025

This growth has been accompanied by a shift in how the company reports its earnings to better align with international standards.

In 2025, Bitpanda began focusing on adjusted EBITDA as its primary performance indicator, achieving a margin of over 30 percent.

This transparency is intended to show potential shareholders that the platform is not just growing in user numbers, which now exceed 7 million, but is also a sustainable and profitable business.

The company’s ability to secure a MiCA licence in Germany and approval from the UK Financial Conduct Authority has also added a layer of regulatory security that many of its competitors lack.

A New Leadership Team Takes The Reins For The IPO

Ahead of the planned listing, Bitpanda has restructured its top-level management to better handle the responsibilities of a public company.

In 2025, co-founder Eric Demuth stepped back from the day-to-day operations as co-CEO to become the Executive Chairman of the Board.

10 years of @Bitpanda_global with the best co-founders I could have hoped for.

I’ve never been a big believer in the concept of “luck.” For me, luck is just about optimizing your chances, maximizing the expected value of an outcome. But if I’m honest, meeting @christiant5r and… pic.twitter.com/zvDhF6bXmf

— Eric Demuth (@eric_demuth) January 3, 2025

This left Lukas Enzersdorfer-Konrad as the sole CEO to lead the company through its stock market debut.

The other original founders, Paul Klanschek and Christian Trummer, had already moved out of executive roles.

This shift provides the company with a more traditional corporate structure that investors often prefer during an IPO.

The new leadership is tasked with maintaining Bitpanda's momentum as it competes for dominance against global exchanges like Binance and Kraken.

How Will The 2026 Crypto IPO Wave Impact The Market

Bitpanda is not the only digital asset firm trying to catch the current wave of market optimism.

Several major players are lining up for their own debuts in 2026, encouraged by a friendlier regulatory environment in the United States and the clear MiCA guidelines in Europe.

🚨 Crypto is going mainstream faster than most expect.

2026 is shaping up to be a massive year for crypto IPOs 👀

1️⃣ Kraken
2️⃣ ConsenSys
3️⃣ BitGo
4️⃣ Animoca
5️⃣ Ledger
6️⃣ Bithumb

Above are lining up with $35B+ in combined valuations. pic.twitter.com/udVijDPj81

— Lucky (@LLuciano_BTC) December 29, 2025

Kraken has already filed a draft registration for an IPO that could value it at 20 billion dollars, while other firms like tZERO and Grayscale Investments are also making moves.

However, the performance of crypto companies that went public in 2025, such as Circle and Gemini, has been varied.

While some saw high initial trading prices, many later saw their shares retreat.

Bitpanda is hoping that its strong footing in the European market and its recent profitability will help it avoid the volatility that affected earlier crypto listings.

data.title: A Game Between Freedom and Clarity: A Fundamental Study of Poland's Crypto Tax System and Regulatory Framework
data.description:
1. Introduction

In late 2025, Poland was embroiled in a fierce debate surrounding a cryptocurrency regulatory bill. According to an official Polish government announcement, on December 9, 2025, the Council of Ministers approved a draft law on the cryptocurrency market submitted by the Minister of Finance and Economy. This bill, which had been rejected by the President on December 2, was resubmitted with the same wording. This stalemate made Poland one of the few EU countries that had not yet completed its domestic legislation supporting the Crypto-Asset Market Regulation Act (MiCA).

data.title: Best Tokens Issued in 2025
data.description:
Author: Dren Hima, Crypto Researcher at Coinchub; Translation: @JinseFinancexz

Token offerings are a stress test. No other aspect of the cryptocurrency space brings the same level of exposure because suddenly, everything comes with a price tag. Project evaluations are determined by the market, and project survival is determined by users.

This is why token offering failures are often predictable. Some projects launch and market brilliantly, but turn out to be deserted when it comes to actual usage. Others launch real products, but their token offerings are amateurish, resulting in persistently thin liquidity and making holding tokens a burden. There's a third type of project that has a good product and offering foundation, but is ruined by poor communication, bizarre unlocking mechanisms, or offering structures that seem to prioritize insider gains.

These differences have become increasingly apparent over the past year.

With increasing regulatory calls, more discerning exchanges, and increasingly fragmented retail investor attention, successful token offerings often belong to projects that appear consistent from multiple perspectives: user growth that doesn't seem artificial, utility that doesn't rely on a future roadmap, a narrative that aligns with mechanism design, and tokens that possess practical functionality. This token offering ranking attempts to capture these qualities. This article is not intended to be a morally sound ranking or a "best technology" list, but rather to reveal which projects will stand up to scrutiny after "entering" the market in 2025. 1. Introduction The term "best token offering" is often simply understood as "the one with the largest price increase." This understanding is acceptable if you only follow market trends; however, it's meaningless if you want to truly understand the logic behind success. This article ranks projects for 2025 based on the quality of their offerings. A successful token issuance is a moment when the token collides with market reality: product strength, distribution mechanism, liquidity design, reputation foundation, and the speed at which the team's public execution capabilities are verified. Price is important, but it is the noisiest yet often the most chaotic signal. Some of the listed projects seem self-evident in hindsight, while others require in-depth analysis of their mechanism design to discover their value. The market can price a narrative in a week, but pricing execution takes much longer—and execution is often reflected earlier in user growth and ecosystem behavior, rather than on price charts. The issuance landscape in 2025 will not have a single "mainstream model," but rather a diverse mix: institutional tokenization, information finance and AI tools, social finance, privacy chains that balance compliance, DeFi applications that hide gas fees and cross-chain operations, decentralized physical networks becoming real infrastructure, and meme economics evolving into structured products. If you want to see which projects are bullish in the next cycle, this article is more valuable than those token lists that flood Twitter's trending topics. It should be noted that "issuance" in this article specifically refers to token issuance, not the launch of a company, product, or protocol. Many of the most successful native crypto platforms in 2025 (including mainstream prediction markets and financial infrastructure providers) did not issue tokens and are therefore not included in this ranking. This article does not attempt to assess which products or companies performed best overall, but rather focuses on how tokens enter the market and whether their issuance is supported by actual utility, user base, execution quality, and reliable delivery capabilities. The following rankings combine results from all scoring dimensions. The score differences between projects are relatively close—there were no absolute winners this year, but rather a cluster competition where top projects were distinguished from others by execution quality.

2、Methodology

A successful launch should create a token market supported by genuine behavior. Therefore, we score projects from multiple dimensions, covering the complete fundamentals of the launch: market acceptance, adoption rate, execution quality, product usability, transparency, liquidity acquisition capabilities, and pragmatic considerations regarding regulatory attitudes. We weight these dimensions to ensure that the ranking is not dominated by a single type of signal.

Adoption rate and execution quality have the highest weights because they are more difficult to fake in the long run.

While market capitalization and price are important, we deliberately control their weighting because they are too sensitive to timing and narrative cycles. Social influence, as a real asset in the crypto space, is considered, but only as supporting evidence, not a core foundation. Utility is the focus of our research. We don't score "utility" by reiterating marketing rhetoric; instead, we assess it by examining launched features, clear user flows, and evidence of a reasonable connection between the token and the product. If a token has been issued but the product remains in the conceptual stage, it is not considered a genuine launch. Transparency is considered a risk filter. Projects will be penalized when the launch design clearly intends to conceal key information, or when communication patterns create unnecessary information asymmetry between insiders and users. Regulatory enforcement is not a "good guy badge," but a pragmatic signal reflecting how a project paves the way for listing, partnerships, and long-term development. The perspective of the EU's Crypto Asset Market Regulatory Scheme (MiCA) is particularly important because 2025 is a turning point for many teams, at least on paper, to no longer consider compliance an option. The choice of registration location and the type of filings a team chooses often reveals its target market positioning. Finally, it should be noted that since this analysis focuses on token issuance, projects that have not issued native tokens are not included in the evaluation (regardless of their product quality or market influence). Data collection ended in the first week of January 2026.

3, Limitations and Interpretation Guidelines

This article reflects the market conditions at a specific point in time. The cryptocurrency market changes rapidly, and the indicators used in this article, such as market capitalization, number of active wallets, market sentiment, exchange listings, and liquidity conditions, may all change significantly in the short term. Therefore, rankings and scores should be considered as an assessment of the issuance quality observed during the data collection period, rather than a permanent judgment on the future development of projects.

The analysis comprehensively uses on-chain data, exchange data, publicly disclosed information, project documents, and data from third-party aggregation platforms. Although these data sources are widely used in the industry, they all have limitations: on-chain data may be incomplete or lead to different interpretations due to methodological differences; exchange report data may vary from platform to platform; social and sentiment data may be affected by bot activity, coordinated manipulation, or platform-specific dynamics. For missing or ambiguous data, we adopt a neutral assumption to avoid over-penalizing projects, but this inevitably introduces estimation risks.

The scoring framework itself is a model designed by Coincub. The category selection, weighting, and standardization methodology reflect our understanding of high-quality crypto project launches in 2025. Adoption rate, utility, transparency, and execution are intentionally given higher weight than short-term price performance or narrative buzz. While this approach aims to reduce noise and speculative bias, it can still be affected by structural or statistical biases. Different weighting settings, alternative definitions of adoption rate, or different interpretations of utility can all produce differentiated ranking results. Some metrics (especially those related to utility and regulatory compliance) require subjective judgment rather than purely mechanical scoring. In such cases, assessments are primarily based on observable product deliverables, documented use cases, and external validation, rather than forward-looking promises. Even so, subjectivity remains in the interpretation process, and different analysts may reasonably differ in their scoring of specific features or disclosures. This article does not constitute investment advice and does not recommend the buying, selling, or holding of any digital assets. Rankings are not price trend predictions, nor do they represent an endorsement of any project. Their core value lies in helping readers understand the differences in execution across various practical dimensions of different projects, rather than predicting returns or assessing individual risk tolerance. Finally, it must be reiterated that the cryptocurrency market is essentially still in an experimental phase. Projects may evolve, fail, transform, or develop in ways that exceed expectations—something no static model can fully capture. This article should be viewed as a structured analytical perspective, not absolute truth. Its value lies in building a framework for evaluating issuance quality, weighing trade-offs, and execution models, rather than treating any rating as an absolute standard. 4. **Key Trends for 2025** User adoption is the initial test of reality. Observing real user flow is crucial before focusing on rankings, narratives, or awards. The number of active wallets is not a perfect indicator, but in a large-scale scenario, its credibility far exceeds that of trading volume or social media buzz. The gap between top-tier and long-tail projects is significant, setting the tone for the subsequent analysis in this article. It's important to note that the project with the highest absolute number of users does not necessarily have the greatest industry influence—Pi Network is a prime example. Not all launch failures stem from the same cause. Some perform poorly due to inappropriate timing, some due to weak products, and others due to unnecessary risks created during execution. When transparency, usability, and regulatory enforcement are aligned, the project's appearance is drastically different from a narrative-driven launch. The enforcement of regulatory compliance is unevenly distributed. Project issuances tend to concentrate in regions where liquidity, listing channels, and institutional partners are most readily available. This concentration of issuance activity in a few EU jurisdictions reflects the teams' strategic considerations regarding reputation building and future market access. While capital is important, it cannot guarantee the quality of issuance. Some of the largest financing projects fail to convert capital into sustainable value, while others achieve greater results with less capital. Financing is merely a process input and should never be regarded as the ultimate goal. Last but not least, market sentiment is valuable, but rarely decisive. A highly bullish community can coexist with weak fundamentals, and strong execution can exist even in the absence of optimism. Sentiment amplifies results, but it cannot create them. Data snapshots show that projects like MemeCore, DoubleZero, and Canton achieved more real results amidst pessimism than bullish tokens with high volume but low trading volume.

5、2025Best Token Issuance List

（1）Canton（CC）——Best Institutional-Grade Issuance

Canton is based in the institutional-grade on-chain finance track, and its privacy protection function is not an option but a necessity.

The most straightforward way to understand Canton is that it's designed for regulated institutions, allowing them to enjoy the advantages of a shared ledger infrastructure without exposing sensitive positions and transaction data globally. This alone defines its user base—either built for this real-world need or unrelated to it. Canton's strength lies in not treating privacy as a decorative add-on, but as a core requirement built upon it. This is directly reflected in the types of institutions willing to connect to the network. The most important signal in 2025 is the DTCC-led initiative to tokenize US Treasury bonds through Canton. Digital Asset calls this a key step towards a viable production path, with pilot programs aiming to launch in 2026. Such progress only occurs when the infrastructure gains professional approval. Broadridge's digital liquidity repository is another important anchor, showcasing the types of workflows Canton seeks to capture—large-scale institutional buybacks and collateral transfers. Broadridge is now the tokenized settlement layer actually used by mainstream market participants. In terms of usability, Canton scores highly because it doesn't paint a vague future vision. The network's core use case is enabling institutions to trade on a shared system without disclosing their full internal state to competitors. Canton also excels in transparency and regulatory enforcement; its entire listing strategy hinges on gaining serious attention from key counterparties. While this imposes limitations, it also builds credibility. Canton tops the list due to its credible partners, clear institutional-grade utility, and execution capabilities that don't rely on retail investor enthusiasm.

（2）Kaito（KAITO）——The Best AI-Native Crypto Platform

Kaito is not trying to become another "AI concept coin," but rather aims to become the interface layer for crypto practitioners to obtain information.

Observing the daily operations of the crypto field, you will find that it is filled with fragmented research, social information streams disguised as valid signals, and a continuous game between genuine insights and traffic bait.

Kaito's product direction directly addresses this pain point, making it seem reasonable. Its effectiveness is not just empty talk: if the product is truly effective, users will become dependent on it. It doesn't require user "faith," it simply requires them to stop opening other tabs simultaneously. Kaito's "InfoFi" framework essentially argues that attention and research have value, therefore a system should be built to allow this value to be manifested, measured, and rewarded within the platform, rather than allowing it to be lost to random social algorithms. Tokens bind incentives to contributions and usage behavior. This is the key to Kaito's superiority over the common "token for tokens' sake" model—if the network can truly coordinate the interests of research contributors and consumers, the token has practical functionality. Kaito scores highly in social media influence and exchange listing performance due to its high-visibility market entry and highly transparent issuance process. Market sentiment also scored highly, as "AI + crypto research" is one of the few narratives that remained popular even during periods of localized market cooling. However, it wasn't exempt in terms of user adoption. While many talk about AI, the key is whether its tools are integrated into daily workflows. Kaito ranks highly here precisely because it appears to be one of the very few releases poised to deliver such everyday practical value by 2025.

（3） Zora (ZORA) —— Best Social Project Launch

If social content can create value, people will trade it, and creators should be rewarded when transactions occur—this is the core idea. Tokenize posts and profiles, transform interactions into on-chain markets, and eliminate friction in the monetization process.

Zora's ranking in the top three is key to its realistic distribution.

Zora's top-three ranking is due to its realistic distribution.

This project benefits from building an ecosystem within existing user communities and cleverly leveraging familiar crypto user behaviors: the cycle of speculation and trading. This is precisely the complexity of evaluating Zora. Critics see a casino-like frenzy, while supporters see a new paradigm for the creator economy. To be fair, it's both. Its core utility isn't "content publishing," but rather converting social attention into a liquidity market—a real use case in the crypto space, even if it might not seem "respectable" to outsiders. Zora scores highly in user adoption because its expansion speed surpasses almost all other sectors when social tokenization is embedded in the right distribution channels. Its social impact score is equally impressive because the project itself is rooted in a community best suited to amplifying such mechanisms. Successful launches aren't always those projects with a strong sense of "ethics," but rather those that accurately map crypto user behavior into a closed product loop.

（4）Midnight (NIGHT)——Best Privacy Chain

Privacy chains often fail for foreseeable reasons: excessive development difficulty, overly closed ecosystems, overly idealistic concepts, or detachment from compliance realities. Midnight reconstructs privacy as a practical tool for selective disclosure, rather than a completely invisible cloak.

The most distinctive element of distribution in 2025 is its distribution structure.

Midnight's "Glacier Drop" model and time-based claiming design demonstrate the team's attempt to avoid the worst-case scenario for distribution dynamics—where short-term arbitrage dominates. Privacy itself is not a use case. Privacy only becomes a use case when developers can build applications that allow users to "prove something without revealing everything." Authentication, compliance proofs, credential management, and private business logic—these are the real markets. Midnight is packaging privacy as an application-layer concept and presenting it within a developer-friendly framework. If its implementation extends to the mainnet launch, this will be one of the most significant privacy technology explorations in recent years.

（5） Sonic (S) — Best Brand Rebranding Case

Although many still consider S a derivative of FTM, it still meets the criteria for release in 2025. But Sonic did something most projects failed to do: it acknowledged that technology alone cannot build an ecosystem, but that incentive mechanisms can play a role.

（6）Rain（RAIN）——Best Prediction Market Protocol

Rain is a decentralized prediction market protocol built on Arbitrum with cross-chain support. It is closer to a "Uniswap for prediction markets" than a gambling application. Any user can create markets, trade results, and provide liquidity without permission, without relying on centralized operators or being geographically restricted.

（7）DoubleZero (2Z)——Best Infrastructure Release

DoubleZero is one of the more honest releases of 2025 because it directly addresses a problem that people constantly complain about but few have actually addressed: network layer performance and data propagation limitations.

This project doesn't pretend that blockchain exists in a vacuum, but rather views blockchain as a distributed system that relies on real-world network connections.

Its core concept is to build a dedicated connectivity layer: aggregating underutilized fiber optic lines to provide a "fast track" for blockchain traffic. Related reports describe it as a new type of physical infrastructure network built around fiber optic contributions, which captures the essence of the project. This is why, despite being a heavy infrastructure type, it scores highly in terms of usability—it doesn't require user "faith," but rather provides validators, RPC service providers, and latency-sensitive systems with the services they actually need. Infrastructure adoption is difficult to measure early on because usage data isn't as readily apparent as retail wallets. This also explains why its number of active wallets isn't in the top five. However, as a launch, it deserves this position because it's one of the few attempts to treat blockchain performance as a physical network issue.

（8）DeFi Apps（HOME）——Best DeFi Starter Products

Most people don't use DeFi because the DeFi experience is like chores. Wallet management, gas fees, cross-chain, signing, switching networks—half the experience is friction and anxiety. DeFi Apps attempt to compress these into an interface closer to "one-click operation".

... Its usability score stems from its product philosophy: simplifying complexity, standardizing operations, and making DeFi tasks feel like application behaviors rather than protocol rituals. If the HOME token is truly used as the core unit driving in-app transactions and incentives, its role becomes meaningful—it prevents the token from becoming an accessory. The life or death of entry-level products depends on sustained usage. A successful launch alone is far from enough; the key is whether users are willing to stay when rewards fade and novelty wears off. Nevertheless, as a project launched in 2025, HOME should still be included on this list because it is one of the very few projects that treats user experience as a core product rather than an afterthought.

（9）MemeCore（M）——Best Native Meme Ecosystem

MemeCore is a real attempt to industrialize Meme token behavior and build a structured ecosystem.

This may sound absurd, but the market continues to prove that Memes are an important engine for liquidity and attention distribution.

MemeCore attempts to capture this value by creating a public blockchain and incentive system specifically for meme creation and the meme economy. Its model directs value back to ecosystem incentives, rather than letting each meme token burn out instantly. It scores highly in the social influence dimension because meme projects that don't capture mindshare perish. Compared to other meme issuances, its utility score is also outstanding—it's not just a token, but an exploration of ecosystem design. Its weakest areas are transparency and long-term trust risk. The meme ecosystem remains shrouded in the shadows of insider advantage, short-term arbitrage, and narrative fragility.

（10） Kite (KITE) —— The Most Futuristic Release

Kite represents a trend in 2025: projects are attempting to build services for agent-based use cases rather than human users.

Its core assumption is that AI agents will autonomously conduct transactions, payment routing, computing power procurement, and service interactions.

If this assumption holds true, the concept of "blockchain as a settlement layer for intelligent agents" will no longer be science fiction. Kite's practicality, while forward-thinking, is not mere rhetoric. The project's design framework around intelligent agent identity and autonomous interaction allows it to rank among the top ten rather than be seen as a purely narrative project. Proof-of-value is still ongoing. The market may be excited about this concept, but continued adoption will depend on whether developers truly build intelligent agent workflows that require an on-chain layer. Kite is one of the few launches attempting to design for next-generation application models.

6、Conclusion: Lessons from 2025

Over the past two years, the token issuance landscape has undergone a profound and subtle change. Our 2024 report pointed out that a number of projects successfully attracted attention and liquidity in a market environment dominated by narrative and volatility. At that time, the signals of adoption were noisy and unclear, and "utility" often only meant "the presence of market participants."

data.title: Vitalik: All the prerequisites for the original Web3 vision are in place.
data.description:
Source: Vitalik, Ethereum founder; Compiled by: Jinse Finance

In 2014, there was a vision: we could have permissionless, decentralized applications that could support finance, social media, ride-hailing, governance, crowdfunding, and even potentially create a complete alternative internet.

Ethereum: Blockchain. A global computer providing shared memory for any application.

Whisper: Data layer. Used to transmit messages that are too costly for the blockchain and do not require consensus.

Swarm: Storage layer. Stores files for long-term access.

Over the past five years, this core vision has sometimes become blurred, with various "metas" and "narratives" taking center stage at different times. But the core vision has never died. In fact, the core technologies supporting it are becoming increasingly powerful.

Ethereum is now a Proof-of-Stake (PoS) mechanism.

Ethereum is scaling, it's cheap, and thanks to the power of ZK-EVM, it's moving towards greater scalability and lower cost. Thanks to ZK-EVM + PeerDAS, the vision of "sharding" is effectively becoming a reality. And L2 can provide additional and different types of speed improvements on top of that. Whisper has now evolved into Waku and already powers many applications (such as Railway and Status, which I use). Even outside of Waku, the quality of decentralized messaging has improved. Fileverse (a decentralized alternative to Google Docs and Sheets) has made significant progress in usability over the past year. IPFS is now very efficient and robust as a decentralized file retrieval method, although storage issues cannot be solved by IPFS alone. Therefore, there is still room for improvement. All the prerequisites for the original Web3 vision are in place, momentum is strong, and will continue to strengthen in the coming years. Therefore, it's time to start building, and to build in a decentralized way. Fileverse is a prime example of doing things the right way: It leverages the strengths of Ethereum and Gnosis Chain: name, account, and permission management, and document registration. It utilizes decentralized messaging and file storage to store documents and propagate changes to them. The application has passed the "walkaway test," https://github.com/fileverse/walk-away-ddocs, meaning that even if Fileverse disappears, you can still retrieve documents and even continue editing them using the open-source UI. This is what we mean by "building your own hammer, a tool you buy once and it's yours, not some 'corposlop' AI dishwasher—the kind that requires you to register a Google account, charges a monthly subscription for extra wash modes, might monitor you, and stops working if you're deemed politically incorrect by some foreign country." If you think this criticism of "corporate garbage" is an exaggeration, then it turns out it's literally a combination of these three cases: A user buys a new dishwasher only to find they have to subscribe to use some of its features. Air fryers, smartwatches, speakers, and trackers involve excessive surveillance. US sanctions have locked down judges of the International Criminal Court in their daily lives, preventing them from living normal lives. In 2014, decentralized applications were just toys, hundreds of times more difficult to use than Web2. By 2026, Fileverse is good enough that I often write documentation and send it to others for collaboration. The resurgence of decentralization is coming, and you can be a part of making it a reality.

data.title: The xAI incident reveals the different understandings of technological transgression between China and the US.
data.description:
Author: Han Qin, CEO of Jarsy, Source: @hqinjarsy

Looking at the different understandings of technological transgression between China and the US from the xAI incident. Why was it the institutional background, rather than personal choice, that determined his fate?

Recently, the Chinese community in Silicon Valley has been discussing Xuechen Li's theft of xAI code. Reactions have been varied: some say it's a matter of personal morality, some talk about educational failures, and some discuss a culture of theft.

But I think these judgments are somewhat superficial. If we shift our perspective from him personally to how different systems understand technological transgression, then this incident becomes an excellent example.

Especially since the understandings of technology, boundaries, and legal responsibility in the two systems of China and the US are almost opposite.

Many elites who have grown up in China assume that technology is neutral, code is knowledge, and knowledge should flow freely. However, this is merely an experiential projection formed within the Chinese context, not a true premise of the American system. In the Chinese institutional environment, technology is often seen as a development resource, not a sovereign asset. The legitimacy of technology flow is often endorsed by development goals. Since development is the fundamental principle, the "flow" of technology is not inherently wrong. The severity of transgressions depends on whether they cause visible damage, cross political red lines, and affect macroeconomic stability. Technology itself is rarely seen as inherently requiring criminal protection. In social practice, many problems occur first, are then internally resolved, and finally handled administratively or civilly. Criminal liability is not the first reaction. This fosters a very typical risk perception: As long as no obvious consequences occur, there is still room for explanation and remediation. Especially in technology organizations, decision-making is seen as a collective act, responsibility is diluted by the organizational structure, and individuals are rarely held directly accountable as independent legal entities. Moreover, this experience has a considerable practical basis in China. Therefore, in Chinese business competition, we often see or hear about "poaching," but rarely about "mergers and acquisitions." The American system, however, understands the same type of behavior almost entirely differently. The first principle is that technology is property and also a national security asset. In the United States, especially in fields such as AI, semiconductors, and algorithms, code is a trade secret, core intellectual property, and sometimes, in specific areas, directly equated with national security resources. In companies like xAI, technology isn't "knowledge," but rather the "protected object" itself. Furthermore, the US legal system highly emphasizes individual liability, which isn't reduced based on organizational position, technological contribution, or level of intelligence. There's no such thing as a narrative of "this is for technological advancement" or "this is for better research" to absolve oneself of responsibility. This is why in US business competition, we hear more about "mergers and acquisitions" and rarely about "poaching." Comparing the Chinese and American systems reveals a crucial difference. The Chinese system trains sensitivity to results: has it caused problems? Will it affect stability? Can it be resolved internally? The American system, on the other hand, trains respect for boundaries: have you authorized it? Have you crossed a non-negotiable line? In China, red lines often appear after the consequences manifest. In the US, red lines exist before the action occurs. Why do these kinds of incidents tend to occur among the smartest people? Because the technological path of China's elite has long emphasized breaking boundaries, optimizing rules, and finding space in gray areas. And when this ability is brought into the American institutional environment, those most prone to overstepping boundaries are precisely those with the strongest boundary-management skills. This isn't because they don't understand the law, but because they have never been truly punished by the system during their upbringing. Therefore, incidents like xAI don't truly reveal the moral problems of certain individuals, but rather that when China's technological elites enter the American system, which prioritizes boundaries and punishes based on subjective intent, major problems are almost inevitable if they continue to rely on a results-oriented risk-taking instinct. Technological globalization has ended; strong intellectual property rights are taking its place.

data.title: Forget about the lackluster performance in 2025; BTC and dollar liquidity have bottomed out simultaneously.
data.description:
Source: Arthur Hayes, Founder of BitMEX; Translated by: Jinse Finance

My divine avatars are all adorable plush toys. During Hokkaido's peak ski season in January and February, I pray to the snow goddess, "Frowny Cloud." The precipitation pattern that dominates the region ensures that in a good season—when the snow is almost relentlessly pouring down—you never see the sun. Thankfully, I also pray to the vitamin goddess, a cute horse toy ;), which provides me with vitamin D3 tablets. While I love snow, not all snow is good and safe. The kind of carefree, hardcore skiing experience I enjoy requires a special kind of snow. Low winds and temperatures between -5 and -10 degrees Celsius at night are prerequisites. This allows new snow to effectively combine with old snow, creating conditions for "bottomless powder." During the day, "sorrowful clouds" block specific wavelengths of sunlight, preventing certain slopes (such as sunny sides) from being "cooked" and thus potentially causing landslides. Sometimes, "sorrowful clouds" abandon us fearless skiers at night. Cold, clear nights create "facets" in the snow layer, which has cooled after warming, forming a persistent weak layer. This phenomenon can persist for a period of time, and when the weight of a skier causes energy transfer leading to its collapse, it can trigger a deadly avalanche. As always, the only way to understand what kind of snow layer buildup "sorrowful clouds" create is to study history. On slopes, we do this by digging huge snow pits and analyzing the different types of snow that fall over time. But since this isn't an article about avalanche theory, our approach in the market is to study charts and the interaction between historical events and price movements. The relationship I hope to explore in this article is that of Bitcoin, Gold, "Stonks" (specifically referring to the large-cap U.S. tech stocks that make up the Nasdaq 100 index), and Dollar Liquidity. Those cryptocurrency opponents who are either Gold bugs or financial elites who believe in "holding stocks for the long term" (my GPA wasn't high enough to attend Professor Seigel's class at Wharton), are ecstatic that Bitcoin will be the worst-performing mainstream asset class in 2025. These gold bugs scoff at crypto: if Bitcoin is seen as a vote against the established order, why hasn't it matched or outperformed gold? Those who tout dirty fiat currency stocks also mock crypto: Bitcoin is just a high-beta plaything on Nasdaq, but it hasn't even caught up in 2025, so what value does cryptocurrency have in any portfolio? This article will present a large "chart feast," along with my annotations to illustrate the synergistic movement of these assets. I believe Bitcoin's performance is entirely as expected. It has followed the wave of fiat currency liquidity, particularly dollar liquidity, as the credit impulse of "Pax Americana" is the most significant force in 2025. Gold is surging because price-insensitive sovereign nations are frantically hoarding it, fearing that remaining in US Treasury bonds will lead to the US stealing their wealth as it did to Russia in 2022. The recent "act of war" against Venezuela will only strengthen countries' desire to store gold rather than US Treasury bonds. Ultimately, the AI ​​bubble and all its supporting industries will not disappear. In fact, US President Trump must double down on state support for all AI-related things because it is the biggest contributor to the empire's GDP growth. This means that even if the rate of dollar creation slows, the Nasdaq can continue to rise because Trump has effectively "nationalized" it. For those who study Chinese capital markets, you know that stocks perform very well in the early stages of nationalization, but then perform extremely poorly because political goals take precedence over capitalists' returns on equity. If the price action of Bitcoin, gold, and stocks in 2025 validates my market pattern, then I can continue to watch the volatile nature of dollar liquidity. As a reminder, my prediction is that Trump will "run the economy fucking hot" by injecting credit. A booming economy will help the Republicans' re-election chances this November. Dollar credit will expand as central bank balance sheets grow, commercial banks lend more to “strategic industries,” and money printing leads to lower mortgage rates. When we combine all this historical analysis, does this mean I can continue to “snowplow” without a care in the world—I mean, actively deploy my newly earned fiat currency and maintain maximum risk exposure? You decide. A Chart That Holds All Over To get started, let’s compare the returns of Bitcoin, gold, and the Nasdaq in the first year of Trump’s second term. How do these assets perform relative to changes in dollar liquidity? I’ll explain in detail later in the article, but the assumption is: if dollar liquidity decreased, these assets should have fallen as well. However, gold and stocks rose. Bitcoin’s performance was exactly as expected. Next, I will explain why gold and stocks have performed exceptionally well despite declining dollar liquidity. [Image of Bitcoin (red), gold (gold), Nasdaq 100 (green), and dollar liquidity (magenta)] My crypto journey began with gold. As the Federal Reserve-funded quantitative easing (QE) intensified in 2010 and 2011, I started buying physical gold coins in Hong Kong. In absolute terms, my size was negligible, but as a percentage of my net worth at the time, it was enormous. Ultimately, I learned a painful lesson about position management when I had to sell my gold coins at a loss to buy Bitcoin in order to conduct cash-futures arbitrage on ICBIT starting in 2013. Fortunately, it ultimately turned out well. Since then, I still hold substantial amounts of physical gold coins and bars in vaults around the world, and gold and silver mining stocks dominate my stock portfolio. Readers might ask, since I'm a devout follower of Lord Satoshi, why hold gold? I hold gold because we are in the early stages of a global trend: central banks are selling US Treasury bonds and buying gold. Furthermore, countries are increasingly using gold to settle trade imbalances. This is also evident when analyzing the US trade deficit. In short, I buy gold because central banks are buying gold. Gold has a 10,000-year history and is the true currency of civilization. Therefore, if reserve managers at major central banks distrust the current dollar-dominated fiat currency financial system, they won't convert their savings into Bitcoin; instead, they will (and are) buying gold. If gold's share of global central bank reserves were to return to 1980s levels, the price of gold would rise to around $12,000. Before you believe this "pie-in-the-sky" prediction, let me demonstrate with visual evidence. The orthodox view of gold in the fiat currency system is that it is an inflation hedge. Therefore, it should roughly track the empire's "manipulated CPI index." The chart below shows that gold has roughly tracked this index since the 1930s. However, since 2008, and especially after accelerating after 2022, the price of gold has risen far faster than inflation. Is gold in the midst of a bubble that will soon leave speculators like me devastated?

If gold is in a bubble, retail investors should flock to it. The most popular way to trade gold is through ETFs, with GLD being the largest. When retail investors buy gold in large quantities, the number of GLD shares issued increases. To compare this phenomenon across different time periods, we must divide the number of GLD shares issued by the price of physical gold. The chart below shows that the ratio is declining rather than rising, meaning a true gold speculation frenzy has not yet materialized. [Image of GLD US outstanding shares divided by the spot gold price] If retail investors aren't driving up gold prices, then who are the price-insensitive buyers? Central bank governors around the world. Over the past two decades, two landmark moments have convinced these monetary gurus that the American currency is only good for wiping your bottom. In 2008, American financial magnates created a global deflationary financial panic. Unlike 1929, when the Federal Reserve relatively allowed credit destruction, this time the Fed reneged on its obligation to maintain the dollar's purchasing power, frantically printing money to "save" certain large financial players. This marked the peak and trough of sovereign nations' holdings of US Treasury bonds and gold. In 2022, President Biden shocked the world by freezing US Treasury bonds held by Russia, the country with the largest nuclear arsenal and the world's largest commodity exporter. If the US was willing to abolish Russian property rights, it could do the same to any weaker or less resource-rich country. Predictably, other countries could not honestly increase their risk of confiscation by holding US Treasury bonds. They began accelerating their gold purchases. Central banks are price-insensitive buyers of gold. If the US president steals your money, it's gone in an instant. As long as gold eliminates counterparty risk, it makes sense to buy it at any price. The ultimate reason for sovereign nations' insatiable appetite for this "savage relic" (gold) is that net trade imbalances are increasingly being settled in gold. The record contraction of the U.S. trade deficit in December 2025 proves that gold is re-establishing its status as a true global reserve currency. More than 100% of the change in the U.S. net trade balance is attributable to gold exports. According to data released by the U.S. Commerce Department on Thursday, the gap between merchandise exports and imports narrowed by 11% from the previous month to $52.8 billion. This narrowed the deficit to its smallest level since June 2020. Exports rose 3% from August to October to $289.3 billion, primarily driven by increased exports of the non-monetary commodity gold. — Source: Financial Times (FT) Gold rises when dollar liquidity declines because sovereign nations are accelerating the restoration of the global gold standard.

Long Duration Loves Liquidity

In every era, there are high-flying technology stocks. During the roaring bull market in the US in the 1920s, RCA (a radio manufacturer) was a hot technology stock.

In the 1960s and 70s, it was IBM producing the new mainframes. Today, AI supercomputing service providers and chip manufacturers are at their peak. Humans are inherently optimists. We love to predict a glorious future: the spending of tech companies today will create a social utopia. For those fortunate enough to own the future by holding equity in such businesses, wealth will fall from the sky. To conjure this certain future in investors' minds, companies burn money and take on debt. When liquidity is cheap, betting on the future becomes easy because abundant cash flow will belong to these courageous entrepreneurs in the future. Therefore, investors are happy to squander worthless cash today on tech stocks in exchange for the opportunity to have even larger cash flows in the future, driving up their price-to-earnings ratios. Thus, tech growth stocks grow exponentially during periods of excess liquidity. Bitcoin is a monetary technology. The value of this technology is only relative to the degree to which fiat currencies depreciate. The decentralized blockchain that achieves Byzantine fault tolerance is a great invention, which in itself guarantees that Bitcoin's value is greater than zero. However, for Bitcoin to approach a value of $100,000, sustained depreciation of fiat currencies is needed. Bitcoin's asymptotic rise stems directly from the explosive growth in the dollar supply following the 2008 global financial crisis. Therefore, I say: When dollar liquidity increases, Bitcoin and the Nasdaq rise. The only problem with this statement is the recent divergence between Bitcoin's price and the Nasdaq. My explanation is: The Nasdaq did not pull back in 2025 despite declining dollar liquidity because AI has been simultaneously "nationalized" by both China and the United States. Tech giants in the AI ​​field have instilled in the leaders of the world's two largest powers the idea that AI will solve everything. AI will reduce labor costs to zero, cure cancer, increase productivity, democratize creativity, and most importantly, achieve global military dominance. Therefore, whichever country "wins" the AI ​​race (and "winning" has no clear definition) will rule the world. China embraced this technological future long ago, perfectly aligning it with its five-year plans. Chinese stock investors frequently sifted through each five-year plan to identify which sectors and stocks would receive cheap government credit. In the US, this kind of analysis is a novelty. Industrial policy is like AI to Trump. Trump drank the AI ​​potion, and now "winning the AI ​​race" is part of his economic agenda. The US government has effectively nationalized any component deemed relevant to "winning." Through executive orders and government investment, Trump is dulling free-market signals so that capital (regardless of actual return on equity) floods into everything AI-related. This is why the Nasdaq is decoupled from Bitcoin and declining dollar liquidity by 2025.

Whether there's a bubble or not, the growth in spending to "win AI" is driving the US economy. Trump campaigned on promises of strong economic growth, so he can't afford to stop now, even if years later he looks back and finds that the return on these expenditures is below the cost of capital.

...

US tech investors should be wary of dreams coming true. US industrial policies aimed at "winning AI" are a perfect way to deplete capital. Trump's (or his successors') political goals will deviate from the best interests of shareholders of companies considered strategic. This is a lesson Chinese stock investors have learned the hard way.

Confucius said, "Reviewing the old helps one understand the new." Clearly, Nasdaq's performance shows that this lesson has not yet been internalized.

Readings below 50 indicate economic contraction. GDP growth has not led to a manufacturing recovery.

I thought Trump was working for white workers? No, Jim, President Clinton sold your jobs to China, then Trump brought factories back, but now the factories are full of Elon Musk's AI robotic arms. Sorry, you've been tricked again, but U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) is hiring!

These charts clearly show that the Nasdaq's rise is supported by the U.S. government.Therefore, as an industry, AI will receive all the capital it needs, even if overall dollar credit growth is weak or shrinks. Therefore, the Nasdaq deviated from my dollar liquidity index in 2025, outperforming Bitcoin. I don't believe the AI ​​bubble is ready to burst. This outperformance will be a feature of global capital markets until it is no longer, or most likely until the Democrats take over the House of Representatives in 2026 (as Polymarket predicts) and possibly the presidency in 2028. If the Republican Party is the party of the Jetsons (futurists), then the Democrats are the party of the Flintstones (conservatives). If gold and the Nasdaq are strong, how can Bitcoin regain its former glory? Dollar liquidity must expand to achieve this. Clearly, I believe this will happen in 2026, so let's explore the specifics. Let's get this heated up. As I mentioned at the beginning of this article, there are three pillars supporting the significant increase in dollar liquidity this year: The Federal Reserve's balance sheet will grow due to money printing. Commercial banks will lend to strategic industries. Mortgage rates will fall due to money printing. As shown in the image above, the Federal Reserve's balance sheet will decline in 2025 due to quantitative tightening (QT). The QT ended in December, at which the Federal Reserve launched its latest money-printing program, called "Reserve Management Purchases" (RMP). I discussed the RMP in detail in a previous article. The chart clearly shows that the balance sheet bottomed out in December. The RMP injects at least $40 billion per month. As more funds are needed to finance the U.S. government, the amount of RMP purchases will increase.

The chart above is the Federal Reserve's weekly indicator of loan growth in the U.S. banking system, called "Other Deposits and Liabilities" (ODL). Reading Lacy Hunt's work brought this to my attention.

Starting in the fourth quarter of 2025, banks issued more loans. When banks issue loans, they create deposits, thus creating money out of thin air. Banks like JP Morgan are happy to lend directly to companies backed by the U.S. government. JP Morgan launched a $1.5 trillion lending facility for this purpose. The process is this: the U.S. government makes an equity investment in a company or provides an underwriting agreement, and then the company applies for loans from JP Morgan or other large commercial banks to expand production. Government support reduces default risk by guaranteeing demand, so banks are happy to create money to finance strategic industries. This is exactly what China does. Credit creation shifts from the central bank to the commercial banking system, at least initially, with a much higher money multiplier. This creates above-trend nominal GDP growth. The U.S. will continue to demonstrate its military power, which requires the commercial banking system to finance the production of weapons of mass destruction. This is why bank credit growth will enjoy a structural upswing in 2026.

Trump is a real estate businessman. He knows how to raise funds for housing construction. The latest statement from "Augustus" Trump is that the government-backed Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac should use the capital on their balance sheets to purchase $200 billion worth of mortgage-backed securities (MBS). This is an increase in dollar liquidity because this capital was idle on the books of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac before Trump's order. If this order succeeds, Trump will not stop there.

data.title: Elon Musk Confirms Grok Will No Longer Undress Images of Real People
data.description:
Digital Safety Walls Rise Around Grok Image Generation

The digital playground where artificial intelligence could once manipulate reality without restriction is quickly shrinking.

Elon Musk’s xAI has been forced to recalibrate the moral compass of its chatbot, Grok, after the tool became a focal point for global controversy regarding non-consensual imagery.
